questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a Vegetable fibre?
questions.question_html: Which of the following is NOT a Vegetable fibre?

questions.question: Plant fibres are made up of
Fibres obtained from animals are generally made up of:
questions.question_html: Fibres obtained from animals are generally made up of:
questions.explanation: Fibres obtained from animals are generally made up of protein threads while that of plants are made up of cellulose. Animal fibers are natural fibers that consist largely of particular proteins. Instances are silk, hair/fur (including wool) and feathers. The animal fibers used most commonly both in the manufacturing world as well as by the hand spinners are wool from domestic sheep and silk.

questions.question: Cellulose: cotton:: protein : ?
questions.explanation: Cotton is chemically a kind of sugar called cellulose. Silk is chemically made up of proteins.

questions.question: Weaving is different form knitting as in weaving.
questions.question_html: Weaving is different form knitting as in weaving.

options.text: Both use two sets of yarn but knit is done by machine
options.html: Both use two sets of yarn but knit is done by machine

options.text: Weaving is done with the machine but knitting by hand
options.html: Weaving is done with the machine but knitting by hand

options.text: Two sets of yarn are used but in knitting only one
options.html: Two sets of yarn are used but in knitting only one

questions.explanation: Weaving is different from knitting as in weaving two sets of yarn is used but in knitting, only one yarn is required. weaving is forming (fabric or a fabric item) by interlacing long threads passing in one direction with others at a right angle to them whereas Knitting is a method by which yarn is manipulated to create a textile or fabric for use in many types of garments.

questions.question: In hot humid condition, which of the following fabrics will you choose for wearing?
questions.question_html: In hot humid condition, which of the following fabrics will you choose for wearing?
questions.explanation: In hot and humid conditions, Silk and Wool tend to retain heat and so are not good options whereas cotton helps in evaporation of sweat and thus making our body cool frequently keeping our body temperature constant. So cotton is best suitable in hot and humid climates

questions.question: The yarn which moves lengthwise during weaving is called ________.
questions.question_html: The yarn which moves lengthwise during weaving is called ________.
questions.explanation: In weaving, the weft (sometimes woof) is the thread or yarn which is drawn through, inserted over-and-under, the lengthwise warp yarns that are held in tension on a frame or loom to create cloth. Warp is the lengthwise or longitudinal thread in a roll, while the weft is the transverse thread.

questions.question: Which one is a type of synthetic fibre?
questions.question_html: Which one is a type of synthetic fibre?
questions.explanation: Cotton, silk, and jute are natural fibres obtained from plants and animals. Nylon is a synthetic fibre manufactured by human beings.

questions.question: Chemical constituent of cotton is ________.
questions.explanation: Chemical Composition of Cotton: Types of plant, soil, and climate effects on the chemical composition of cotton fibers and the number of different elements of the cotton. Cellulose is the main component of cotton and it consists of 94% of 100%.

questions.question: The carding machine is used to:

options.text: Comb loose wool into a sheet

questions.explanation:
After shearing and scouring, the cleaned hair is sent to a factory where the hair of different textures are separated or sorted. This is called sorting. 
Small fluffy fibres called burrs are picked out from the hair. The fibres are scoured again and dried. 
Now the wool is ready to draw into fibres.
Next, the hair is sent into a 'carding' machine. 
it combs the loose wool fibres into a sheet. This sheet is twisted into a rope or silver. This silver is twisted and stretched into a yarn.
